title: Silicon Carbide Converters and MEMS Devices for High-temperature Power Electronics: A Critical Review
authors_or_org: Xiaorui Guo, Qian Xun, Zuxin Li, Shuxin Du (School of Engineering, Huzhou University, China)
claim_supported: SiC devices reach theoretical junction temperatures near 600C vs silicon's 150C ceiling; commercial SiC MOSFET/BJT/JFET devices operate reliably to ~210C with lab prototypes to 727C; four unresolved bottlenecks named: gate-drive design, high-temp current sensing, cross-temperature parameter matching, and packaging maturity.
notes: PMC full text fetched directly this session. Author affiliation (Huzhou University, China) confirmed via separate WebSearch, not the article page itself.

title: Radiation Tolerance Testing Methodology of Robotic Manipulator Prior to Nuclear Waste Handling
authors_or_org: Kaiqiang Zhang, Chris Hutson, James Knighton, Guido Herrmann, Tom Scott (University of Bristol; University of Manchester)
claim_supported: A KUKA iiwa 7 LBR800 industrial robot arm failed after accumulating 164.55(+/-1.09) Gy of Co-60 gamma exposure, with failure in an optical encoder in the end effector; argues strategic use of standard (non-rad-hardened) components with planned replacement schedules can be more cost-effective than specialized rad-hard alternatives for some decommissioning tasks.
locator: Frontiers in Robotics and AI, Vol. 7, Article 6 (Feb 6, 2020)
notes: UK-based authors; used as general technical-feasibility source for radiation-tolerant industrial control, not counted toward Asia mix.

title: 中子辐射导致的SiC功率器件漏电增加特性研究 (Study on characteristics of neutron-induced leakage current increase for SiC power devices)
authors_or_org: Peng Chao, Lei Zhi-Feng, Zhang Zhan-Gang, He Yu-Juan, Ma Teng, Cai Zong-Qi, Chen Yi-Qiang
claim_supported: 14 MeV neutron irradiation (fluence 1.18e11 cm^-2) of SiC Schottky barrier diodes and MOSFETs: SBD forward I-V is stable but reverse leakage increases significantly (deep-level defect at Ec-1.034 eV lowers Schottky barrier); MOSFET gate leakage increases ~3.3x at 15V gate voltage due to donor-type oxide defects -- direct evidence that SiC is not automatically radiation-immune even though it is more temperature-tolerant.
locator: Acta Physica Sinica (物理学报), Vol. 72, Issue 18, Article 186102 (2023)
notes: Official CAS journal host (wulixb.iphy.ac.cn) fetched directly this session; title, authors, DOI, and article number confirmed from the article page itself.

Named performer institutions: University of Dayton, University of Arkansas, Arizona State University, Penn State University, Stanford University, Boeing.
